seoTitle: How to Create Folders in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Organize a cluttered Microsoft Loop workspace by using subpages as digital folders, nesting related pages and dragging files between them.
intro: Microsoft Loop doesn't have traditional folders, but subpages work the same way — letting you group related content into nested, collapsible sections. This guide shows how to build a folder-style structure using subpages and move existing pages into it.

steps.heading: Open your workspace directory
steps.body: Open your Microsoft Loop app and select the working directory you want to organize.

steps.heading: Find the parent page
steps.body: Go to the left sidebar content tree and locate the specific document you want to organize under.

steps.heading: Open the subpage options
steps.body: Hover over the title, click the three dots icon, and select 'Add New Subpage' from the dropdown list.

steps.heading: Name your folder
steps.body: Enter a descriptive folder name and press Enter to lock in the new subpage boundary.

steps.heading: Move existing pages into it
steps.body: Click and hold external page nodes in the sidebar and drag them into your new subfolder to organize related content.

faq.question: Does Microsoft Loop have a dedicated folder feature?
faq.answer: Not exactly — subpages act as the folder equivalent, letting you nest related pages under a parent page in the sidebar.

faq.question: Can I nest folders multiple levels deep?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create multiple layers of sub-nodes to group distinct content types as deeply as your project needs.

faq.question: Can I collapse folders in the sidebar to reduce clutter?
faq.answer: Yes, expanding and collapsing multi-tiered workspace layouts helps keep the active page list scannable.

faq.question: What happens to a page's content when I move it into a subfolder?
faq.answer: Dragging a page into a subpage location reorganizes its place in the sidebar tree without altering its content.
